evidence_L1: Read-only default with explicit permission escalation (least-privilege); OS-level sandboxing (bubblewrap/seatbelt) as a containment boundary; auto-mode circuit breaker halts and escalates after 3 consecutive or 20 total denials (iteration limits w/ fallback + agent circuit breaker); model-based guardrail classifiers (closed-loop). ~5/9 classes on the critical-path internal deployment with measured effectiveness (sandboxing cut permission prompts 84%).
evidence_L2: no public evidence (single-agent tool; no population/interaction-graph monitoring)
evidence_L3: Permission prompts as approval gates, human override/kill, and auto-mode as an explicit tiered-autonomy tier delegating approvals to classifiers with escalation on denial streaks. ~4/9 classes; escalation SLA not published, so held at 0.25.
evidence_L4: Managed settings, deny rules, and managed hooks (policy-as-code gates); OpenTelemetry audit events (semantic observability/golden signals); CI/CD release gates and adversarial validation across real coding workflows (evals in CI/CD); internal incident log of agentic misbehaviors (layer-coded postmortems). ~5/10 classes with measured effectiveness, supporting 0.50.

evidence_L1: Guidance to program strict limits on agent actions (closed-loop guardrails), DLP, and running agents under dedicated least-privilege identities rather than broad user accounts (least-privilege tool access). Partial L1; coverage per-tenant, unmeasured.
evidence_L3: Detailed logs of every trigger, decision, and action (audit/provenance); layered approval workflows separating experimentation from production (approval gates, tiered autonomy). ~3/9 classes; no effectiveness metrics.
evidence_L4: Zoned governance with layered environments and approval workflows (progressive rollout), DLP policies (policy-as-code), dedicated least-privilege agent identities (workload identity). Partial; documented as capability guidance, fleet coverage not measured.

evidence_L1: Agent Gateway is a runtime enforcement point intercepting tool calls to enforce access-control policy and Model Armor inspection (closed-loop guardrails); native IAM granular least-privilege access. Partial L1; per-agent coverage not measured.
evidence_L2: no public evidence (a unified Agent Registry exists but no interaction-graph/cascade monitoring)
evidence_L3: Comprehensive audit logging and per-agent identity giving a clear audit trail (provenance); operational-oversight monitoring of agent health. ~2-3/9 classes; no drill/SLA metrics.
evidence_L4: Per-agent SPIFFE-formatted Agent Identity in IAM (workload identity), Agent Gateway policy enforcement (policy-as-code), Agent Registry, and performance/health monitoring (observability). Strong specific match on workload identity; ~4/10 classes but coverage not measured, so held at 0.25.

evidence_L1: Built-in guardrails enforce agent compliance at every step; reasoning stage separated from action execution to reduce hallucination (closed-loop guardrails, uncertainty-aware control); OpenAI safety tooling integrated. Multiple classes across 350+ deployments.
evidence_L3: Automated-resolution model implies handoff to humans for unresolved cases; safety criterion gates responses. Human escalation path documented (partial L3).
evidence_L4: Fleet-level measurement across 350+ deployments (containment ~72%, automated-resolution ~52%) with defined Relevant/Accurate/Safe criteria and ongoing optimization. Extended golden signals / evals (partial).

evidence_L1: Sensitive operations (refunds, identity verification) executed in deterministic code rather than model discretion; least-privilege integrations. Multiple guardrail classes on production agents.
evidence_L3: Escalations are context-preserving warm transfers delivering conversation history/customer data/resolution attempts to human agents before handoff (escalation gate, partial L3).
evidence_L4: Agent Operating Procedures compiled to structured logic; integrations, guardrails and versioning controlled through Git (policy-as-code + version control); SOC 2, data residency. 1-2 of 10 ops classes.

evidence_L1: Negative anchor case. BC Civil Resolution Tribunal (Moffatt v. Air Canada, 2024) found the airline 'did not take reasonable care to ensure its chatbot was accurate' after it invented a bereavement-fare policy. No guardrail/grounding mechanism evidenced; hallucination reached the customer unchecked.
evidence_L3: No effective supervisory/escalation control evidenced; tribunal held company liable, treating chatbot output as unsupervised website content.
evidence_L4: no public evidence

evidence_L1: GPT-4 with retrieval-augmented generation grounded in enterprise data across 1,300 topics (anti-hallucination grounding, scoped knowledge access). Partial guardrail classes.
evidence_L3: Documented escalation gate: only ~3% of queries are escalated to human agents; remainder resolved autonomously (tiered autonomy, partial L3).
evidence_L4: Measured operations: ~40,000 queries/day, 13M+ conversations resolved at a reported 97% success rate; volume and success monitored (golden signals / semantic observability, partial).

evidence_L1: Agents configured with explicit goals and guardrails to keep them on-policy; scoped tool/knowledge access per brand. Multiple guardrail classes on production agents, unmeasured fleet coverage.
evidence_L3: ADT agent runs with 'tight guardrails and dense human-in-the-loop review'; escalation/handoff to human staff documented on critical-path deployments (partial L3).
evidence_L4: Measured outcomes (WeightWatchers agent ~70% of sessions, 4.6/5 CSAT); Agent SDK with versioning and analytics/trust tooling (semantic observability, partial).

evidence_L1: Cloud coding agent runs in a sandboxed ephemeral environment with a configurable firewall allowlist (least-privilege network/tool access), built-in security protections, and duplicate/content filtering. Partial coverage of L1 classes (least-privilege tool access, closed-loop guardrails); coverage not publicly measured.
evidence_L2: no public evidence of interaction-graph/population/cascade monitoring
evidence_L3: Agent works on a branch and cannot merge without human PR approval (approval gate); enterprise audit-log fields record exactly what the agent did and on whose behalf (provenance); admins have a unified AI-controls view and can disable agents (override). ~3/9 classes, no measured escalation SLA.
evidence_L4: Enterprise AI-governance controls: policies/config in a unified admin view, 24-hour agent-session activity view, audit logging, and responsible-AI evals via Azure AI Foundry (evals in CI). Agent acts under an on-behalf identity. Partial; measured fleet coverage not shown.

evidence_L1: Prompt guardrails plus a composite @duo-developer service-account identity that limits access (least-privilege); admins choose which agents/flows/models are allowed and where (scoped guardrails). Partial L1; coverage unmeasured.
evidence_L3: human_approval_required:true forces a human to confirm irreversible actions (merge/deploy) = explicit approval gates; audit-log integration captures agent invocations, tool calls, and outputs (provenance); git version control provides rollback. ~3-4/9 classes.
evidence_L4: Flows run inside CI/CD with pipeline/MR/change-window guardrails (evals/gates in CI/CD), @duo-developer workload identity, audit-log evidence, and flow sessions with CI job logs (observability). Partial L4; measured coverage not published.

evidence_L1: Multi-agent orchestration with bounded workflows (up to ~400 steps) and an accuracy-first design given 'billion-dollar decision' stakes; grounded on proprietary data estate (iteration-limit + closed-loop guardrail classes, partial).
evidence_L2: no public evidence (multi-agent orchestration exists, but no documented emergence/contagion/population controls)
evidence_L3: Human oversight over analytical outputs with source citations/provenance back to filings and ratings (audit-trail/provenance + approval-gate classes). Unmeasured.
evidence_L4: Serverless multi-agent orchestration on Amazon Bedrock with document-processing pipelines; measured production signals (>1M tokens/day, 30% faster task completion, 100k+ interactions) (observability + golden signals, partial).

evidence_L1: Assistant operates within customer-service scope with fallback to humans; firm publicly acknowledged hallucinations/quality issues on complex edge cases and added guardrails/human handoff (closed-loop guardrail + fallback classes, partial).
evidence_L3: Tiered handling with escalation to human agents for complex cases; in 2025 the firm re-introduced human agents so customers can always reach a human (tiered-autonomy / escalation class). Unmeasured coverage.
evidence_L4: Extensively measured golden signals in production: 2.3M conversations, resolution time 11->2 min, 25% fewer repeat inquiries, CSAT parity, NPS tracking (semantic observability + extended golden signals, partial). No evidence of CI evals, atomic rollback, or chaos drills.

evidence_L1: Cross-functional governance board governs data access and model vetting; a model cannot be used without registration in the control process, and use cases must pass controls and adequate testing. Guardrails + least-privilege data access evidenced (partial); secondary-source reporting.
evidence_L3: Mandatory control/approval process before an AI model can be used; audit logging cited as part of the governance board's remit (approval-gate + audit-trail classes). Unmeasured; reported via secondary sources.
evidence_L4: Model registration in a formal control process, continuous monitoring, and ROI measurement described; internally built platform gives data-governance/deployment control. Partial policy-gate + observability; no evidence of CI evals, chaos drills, or per-agent workload identity. FLAG: mostly secondary reporting.

deployment: AI Jim / Maya claims and quote agents
evidence_L1: Deterministic policy/rules layer sits in front of the model with runtime evaluation on responses, rules-based coverage checks, anti-fraud checks, and confidence thresholds that trigger fallback (closed-loop guardrail + uncertainty-observer + fallback classes). FLAG: mechanism detail partly from secondary case studies.
evidence_L3: Complex/high-value/questionable claims auto-route to a human expert (tiered autonomy + behavioral escalation) with warm transfer preserving full context; every conversation/tool-call/output persisted (audit trail). Lemonade reports 96% of FNOL taken by bots and 55% of claims fully automated (measured autonomy split).
evidence_L4: Runtime evaluation on every response, ML fraud/severity models, and audit trails persisted to the data warehouse on a defined schedule; measured production automation rates (semantic observability + golden signals, partial).

deployment: GS AI Assistant / GS AI Platform
evidence_L1: All AI traffic passes a secure compliance gateway applying prompt filtering, data anonymization, and policy checks; role-based access restricts tool/data reach (least-privilege + closed-loop guardrail classes). Partial coverage evidenced across ~46,500 employees.
evidence_L3: Human-in-the-loop plus audit logs; for autonomous agents (Claude-built accounting/compliance agents), autonomous execution above defined thresholds still requires human sign-off (tiered autonomy + approval gate + audit trail). Unmeasured publicly.
evidence_L4: Compliance gateway (policy-as-code-style checks), audit logging (observability), and phased rollout from ~10k to 46.5k employees (progressive rollout); governance infrastructure embedded before scaling. Partial; no evidence of CI evals or chaos drills.

evidence_L1: Deterministic NLU/ML design deliberately avoids free-form LLM generation; responses constrained to curated, compliant answers vetted for 100% accuracy, with fallback to human specialists (closed-loop guardrail + iteration-fallback classes). Note: largely non-generative, so 'agent' status is limited.
evidence_L3: Escalation/handoff to a human specialist when Erica lacks a good answer (tiered autonomy); transcripts retained with PII masked and a team reviews conversations (audit/provenance + oversight meta-monitoring). Partial.
evidence_L4: Continuous conversation review and refinement, 90-day retention with PII masking, and measured scale (3B+ interactions, 58M/month) (observability + golden signals, partial). No public evidence of CI evals, progressive rollout tooling, or chaos drills.

evidence_L1: Agent operates over a monitored signal pipeline (80M+ signals/day) to detect emerging fraud patterns within a scoped rule-generation task (closed-loop guardrail + scoped-access classes, partial).
evidence_L3: Strong approval gate: the agent proposes new detection rules but the fraud-analytics team checks and signs off before anything goes into production (approval gate + human-in-the-loop). Governed under a published 'Approach to Adopting AI'.
evidence_L4: Agent generates the detection-rule code (policy-as-code style) with human sign-off gating deployment; measured impact (scam losses down 20% H1 2026; ~75% of card-fraud rules built/updated with the agent). Built on Snowflake data cloud (progressive/gated rollout + observability, partial).

evidence_L1: Privacy-orchestration layer scrubs and tokenizes PII on-premise before any LLM call, guaranteeing zero PII to the model; queries pre-processed/anonymized (least-privilege / data-minimization + closed-loop guardrail classes). Strong but partial class coverage.
evidence_L3: Banking-grade logging/provenance of interactions for compliance (audit-trail class). NOTE: reporting emphasizes 'zero human handoffs', so tiered-autonomy/escalation evidence is weak; scored on audit/provenance only.
evidence_L4: Engineered privacy-orchestration/tokenization pipeline with measured production scale (245.4M interactions in 2024, zero PII incidents) and beta->full progressive rollout (semantic observability + golden signals + progressive rollout, partial).

evidence_L1: Firm built guardrails around testing and bias mitigation; every use case passes a structured eval framework before deployment and outputs are constrained to curated/approved content. Guardrail + testing mechanism classes evidenced on production advisor agents; no evidence of budget caps, circuit breakers, or least-privilege tool scoping documented publicly.
evidence_L3: Human-in-the-loop is mandatory: Debrief generates meeting notes/emails only with client consent, and the advisor reviews and edits every output before sending (approval gate); notes saved to Salesforce give provenance/audit trail. Approval-gate + audit-trail classes evidenced; unmeasured.
evidence_L4: Documented eval framework systematically tests models against real-world scenarios with human-expert grading for accuracy/coherence prior to release (evals-before-deploy). Partial CI-style evals; no public evidence of progressive rollout w/ atomic rollback, chaos drills, or error-budget-modulated autonomy.

evidence_L1: Constellation architecture: a primary conversational model plus 21 specialist supervisor models that block unsafe output (closed-loop safety guardrails, uncertainty-aware observers, circuit-breaker on unsafe content) on all agents.
evidence_L2: no public evidence (multi-model ensemble is internal to a single agent, not a multi-agent population; no emergence/contagion mechanisms documented)
evidence_L3: Strongest L3 case: supervisor models continuously oversee the talking model (oversight meta-monitoring); clinically proven guideline escalation protocols transfer risky calls to human nurses (tiered autonomy, algedonic escalation, approval/override). Multiple classes across all agents.
evidence_L4: Every agent passes a three-step validation (licensure verification, development testing, clinical review by 6,000+ nurses / 300+ physicians) before release; 180M+ interactions monitored. Evals-as-gating (partial ops).

deployment: Clara smart-audit agents / Workbench multi-agent platform

evidence_L4: Built on Azure with Agent 365/Copilot governance; standardizes/automates audit procedures with staged rollout of agents over 12 months (policy governance + progressive rollout + observability, partial).

evidence_L1: Agents deployed under Deloitte's Trustworthy AI principles (robust, reliable, secure) with rigorous testing/QA before deployment (guardrail + testing classes, partial). Used internally for finance/expense processes.
